feedback.text: Greetins, i write my feedback to encourage to include in the bill the requset of the Stop Killing Games Initiative. Their Europen Citizen Initiative "Stop Destroy Videogames" is very popular and supported by public opinion, reaching 1.400.000 signatures in one year. The movement and the initiative amis to protect the rights of the consumer to own the games that they've bought, even when the company publishing the games decides to shut them down. Unfortunaley, a lot of games today are build in a way that forces to connect to the company's server in order to play it, and when they decides that it's not worth to support the game anymore, the server is turned off and became impossible to play it. It's not that hard to release the software of the server of the game they decided to not support anymore, so that the community of that game can host their own server and continue to play, but companies prefers to not do that, creating artificial scarcity at the expense of the consumer. This is especially problematic when the games is obtained with a purchase, writing that you buy the game, since the consumers thinks that they own game and is ther propierty, but then the compay can take the game whatever they whant leaving the consumer with nothing. As european citizen we want that this abusive practice ends, requiring videogame companies to have end of life plans for games that requires to connect to their infrastructure, giving the consumer alternatives to still play the games that they own.
